evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Internally consistent: boto3 client with endpoint_url=https://<ACCOUNT_ID>.r2.cloudflarestorage.com, region_name='auto', generate_presigned_url(ClientMethod='put_object') — all standard boto3 S3 API surface and matches commonly documented R2 boto3 pattern. No version claim stated but methods are consistent across backend and tests.
evidence.executability: Bash, python, jsx blocks parse cleanly. Backend code imports are complete; module-level s3 client constructed at import. Test file references backend.app which requires backend/ to be a package (missing __init__.py is a trivial fix). Frontend CRA install is heavy but feasible in sandbox. No ngrok/sudo patterns.
evidence.completeness: (a) Cloudflare dashboard path given; (b) pip & npm install bash blocks present; (c) .env template with exact names (CF_R2_ACCOUNT_ID, AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, R2_REGION, R2_ENDPOINT, BUCKET_NAME, PRESIGN_EXPIRES) and code uses these same names; (d) FastAPI code fenced; (e) React component fenced; (f) curl + uvicorn test commands and pytest/Jest tests present. All six present.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Multiple official Cloudflare docs URLs (developers.cloudflare.com/r2/...) cited inline next to relevant code (boto3 example, presigned URLs, CORS, tokens). Also boto3 official docs and FastAPI official docs. A couple third-party links (escape.tech, openai help) but official anchors dominate and are placed near code.

rubric_notes: docs_unavailable=true: long-tail handling applied. Internal consistency strong; no version pin so capped at 4 for correctness.
